title: Build wireless channel simulator
description: Python module simulating LoRa radio propagation. Features: (1) Distance-based path loss model, (2) SF/BW impact on range and data rate, (3) Packet collision detection (simultaneous TX), (4) Configurable packet loss/BER, (5) Propagation delay, (6) Duty cycle tracking per node, (7) SNR/RSSI calculation. Should support multiple nodes in a topology.

title: Validate STM32WL memory budget
description: Build minimal Zephyr+IPv6+CoAP on nucleo_wl55jc. Run west build -t ram_report and rom_report. Target: <50KB RAM, <220KB flash. If exceeded, evaluate RIOT OS fallback. This is a go/no-go gate for Zephyr on STM32WL.

title: Spec: Design 6LoWPAN over LoRa adaptation layer
description: Listed as 'open question' but foundational. LoRa is not 802.15.4 — Zephyr's 6LoWPAN assumes 802.15.4 MAC semantics (ACKs, CSMA-CA timing, frame formats). Need concrete adaptation layer design covering: frame format mapping, ACK handling, timing parameters, integration with SCHC.
close_reason: Resolved: Use SCHC (RFC 8724) instead of 6LoWPAN IPHC. No 802.15.4 anywhere. Stack: IPv6 → SCHC → LICHEN Link Layer → LoRa PHY. Zephyr requires custom L2. Updated spec/03-adaptation.md.

title: Spec: Define time synchronization mechanism
description: Time sync is listed as 'open question' but is critical for: replay protection (SeqNum wrapping), SenML timestamps, message TTL expiration, scheduled check-ins, OSCORE sequence numbers. Options mentioned: NTP over CoAP, GPS, DIO piggyback. Need to pick one and specify protocol.
close_reason: Resolved: Hierarchical time sources (GPS > gpsd > NTS > Roughtime > mesh peer > none). Stratum propagated via DIO Time Option. Constrained nodes gracefully degrade to monotonic counters. Updated spec/09-packets-timing.md.

title: Spec: Define truncated Ed25519 algorithm
description: The spec says 'we use a deterministic scheme where the second half is derived from the first' for truncated Ed25519 signatures but provides no concrete algorithm. This is non-standard cryptography with no security analysis. Need to either: (1) specify exact derivation algorithm with security proof, or (2) switch to standard ECDSA approach mentioned in 8.4. Violates 'no hand-rolled crypto' principle.
close_reason: Resolved: Use Schnorr (e₁₂₈, s) variant - 48 bytes, well-known construction, 128-bit security. Added relay-mutable vs signed field separation and signature caching. Updated spec/06-security.md.

title: Announce scheduler and transmission
description:
Implement periodic announce transmission.

AnnounceScheduler class:
- __init__(node_identity, interval=300)
- async start() -> None
- async stop() -> None
- build_announce() -> AnnounceMessage
- async announce_loop()

Behavior:
- Wait ANNOUNCE_INTERVAL + random jitter
- Build announce with current seq_num
- Sign with node's private key
- Broadcast to neighbors
- Increment seq_num

Seq_num persistence:
- Persist to flash on increment
- Load on startup
- Ensure monotonic across reboots

Integration with Node class main loop.

title: Unified gradient table
description:
Implement unified gradient table per spec section 11.

GradientEntry dataclass:
- destination: IID or IPv6Address
- next_hop: link-local address
- hop_count: int
- seq_num: int
- source: 'announce' | 'rrep' | 'data'
- expires: timestamp

GradientTable class:
- lookup(dst) -> GradientEntry | None
- update(entry) -> None
- expire_old() -> None
- __len__() -> int

Entry priority: announce/rrep > data
Same priority: prefer lower hop_count

Populated by:
- Announce processing (section 9)
- RREP processing (section 10)
- Passive learning from forwarded data

Max entries: 64 (configurable). LRU eviction.

title: Announce message format and processing
description:
Implement announce routing per spec section 9.

Announce message (~92 bytes):
- Type, Flags, Hop Count, Seq Num
- Originator IID (8 bytes)
- Public Key (32 bytes)
- Signature (48 bytes)
- Optional app data

AnnounceMessage dataclass with to_bytes/from_bytes.

process_announce(announce, from_neighbor):
1. Verify Schnorr signature
2. Check for stale (seq_num <= existing)
3. Install/update gradient entry
4. Forward if hop_count < MAX_ANNOUNCE_HOPS

Parameters:
- ANNOUNCE_INTERVAL: 300 sec
- MAX_ANNOUNCE_HOPS: 15
- GRADIENT_TIMEOUT: 600 sec
- ANNOUNCE_JITTER: 0-30 sec

title: Hybrid routing decision logic (RPL + LOADng)
LOADng route error handling (RERR)
description:
Implement LOADng route error handling per spec section 9.7.

Link failure detection:
- TX failure (no ACK at link layer) triggers RERR
- Only if route was being used (not stale)

RERR generation:
- Create RERR with unreachable destination
- Send toward affected sources (nodes that were using this route)

RERR processing:
- Invalidate cached routes through broken link
- Propagate RERR if I had downstream users of this route

RouteError class:
- on_link_failure(next_hop) -> None
- process_rerr(rerr_msg) -> None

Track active flows to know who to notify on failure.

title: LOADng route discovery (RREQ/RREP handling)
description:
Implement LOADng route discovery per spec section 9.4-9.5.

RREQ Originator:
- Check cache first, skip discovery if route exists
- Generate RREQ with seq_num, hop_limit
- Broadcast to neighbors
- Start RREQ_WAIT_TIME timer
- On timeout: retry with expanded hop_limit (expanding ring)
- After RREQ_RETRIES: fail

RREQ Forwarder:
- Check if I am destination -> send RREP
- Check if RREQ already seen (by originator+seq) -> drop (suppression)
- Record reverse route to originator
- Decrement hop_limit, rebroadcast if > 0

RREP Handler:
- Follow reverse path to originator
- At each hop: install forward route to destination
- At originator: route ready, send queued packets

RouteDiscovery class:
- async discover(destination) -> RouteEntry | None
- pending_discoveries: dict[dest, Future]
- rreq_seen: set[(originator, seq)] for suppression

Expanding ring search: start HL=4, then 8, then 15.
Rate limit: RREQ_RATELIMIT = 10/min.

title: LOADng route cache implementation
description:
Implement LOADng route cache per spec section 9.6.

Route Entry:
- destination: IPv6 address
- next_hop: IPv6 address (link-local)
- hop_count: int
- metric: int
- seq_num: int
- valid_until: timestamp

RouteCache class:
- lookup(destination) -> RouteEntry | None
- add(entry) -> None
- remove(destination) -> None
- refresh(destination) -> None (extend validity)
- expire_old() -> None

Parameters from appendix-loadng.md:
- ROUTE_CACHE_SIZE: 32
- ROUTE_TIMEOUT: 300 sec
- ROUTE_REFRESH: 60 sec

LRU eviction when cache full.
Unit tests for lookup, expiry, eviction.

title: LOADng message types (RREQ, RREP, RERR)
description:
Implement LOADng control messages per spec section 9.

ICMPv6 type 158, codes:
- 0: RREQ (Route Request)
- 1: RREP (Route Reply)
- 2: RERR (Route Error)
- 3: RACK (Route Acknowledgment)

RREQ fields:
- Hop Limit, Seq Num, Metric, Flags
- Originator Address (128 bits)
- Destination Address (128 bits)

RREP fields:
- Hop Count, Seq Num, Metric, Flags
- Originator Address
- Destination Address

RERR fields:
- Flags, Error Code
- Unreachable Address

Dataclasses for each message type with to_bytes/from_bytes.
Use ICMPv6 framing.

title: Evaluate LOADng for peer-to-peer mesh routing
description:
Evaluate LOADng (Lightweight Ad hoc On-Demand - Next Generation) as alternative/complement to RPL for peer-to-peer mesh traffic.

Problem with RPL-only:
- Lateral routing goes through common ancestor (inefficient)
- Two adjacent leaf nodes route up and back down
- At LoRa duty cycles, this wastes precious airtime
- Thread (Google/Apple/Amazon) rejected RPL for this reason

Proposed hybrid:
- RPL: traffic to/from border router (tree-shaped, RPL's strength)
- LOADng: peer-to-peer within mesh (reactive, efficient lateral paths)

LOADng overview (draft-clausen-lln-loadng):
- Reactive routing (routes built on demand)
- RREQ/RREP/RERR messages
- Designed for LLNs (Low-power Lossy Networks)
- Simpler than AODV, optimized for constrained devices
- Bidirectional route discovery

Evaluation tasks:
1. Read LOADng draft thoroughly
2. Analyze message overhead vs RPL DAO overhead  
3. Consider route discovery latency (first packet delay)
4. Evaluate memory requirements (route cache vs routing table)
5. Prototype in Python simulator
6. Compare: RPL-only vs LOADng-only vs hybrid

Routing decision logic for hybrid:
- if dst in mesh_prefix: use LOADng
- else: use RPL toward border router

Questions to answer:
- Can LOADng and RPL coexist cleanly?
- How do we handle the case where LOADng route doesn't exist but RPL can reach it via root?
- What's the RREQ/RREP overhead at LoRa rates?

References:
- draft-clausen-lln-loadng
- RFC 3561 (AODV)
- Thread MLE specification (for comparison)
close_reason: Decision made: Hybrid RPL+LOADng. Spec updated. Implementation issues will be created separately.

title: Topology change and re-routing test
description:
Test RPL adaptation to topology changes.

Scenario 1 - Link failure:
1. 5-node mesh, stable DODAG
2. Use chaos API to partition B from C
3. Verify: C, D, E find alternate parent or lose connectivity
4. Remove partition
5. Verify: DODAG re-converges

Scenario 2 - Node failure:
1. Stable DODAG
2. Kill node C
3. Verify: D, E re-parent through alternate path (if exists) or go offline
4. Restart C
5. Verify: C rejoins, D, E may re-parent through C

Scenario 3 - Mobile node:
1. Stable DODAG
2. Move node E from near D to near B
3. Verify: E re-parents to B, rank improves

Timing: track convergence time, should be reasonable (< 30 seconds).

title: LICHEN Node class (main integration)
description:
Main Node class that integrates all protocol layers.

Node class:
- __init__(identity, radio, config)
- Owns all layer instances
- Manages lifecycle (start, stop)

Layer instances:
- radio: Radio (SimRadio or real)
- link: LinkLayer
- schc: SchcContext
- ipv6: IPv6Layer
- rpl: RplEngine
- coap: aiocoap Site + LichenTransport

Packet flow (RX):
radio.receive() -> link.receive() -> schc.decompress() ->
ipv6.process() -> rpl.process() or coap.process()

Packet flow (TX):
coap/app -> ipv6.send() -> schc.compress() ->
link.send() -> radio.transmit()

Async main loop:
- Receive task: continuously receive and process
- Trickle task: RPL DIO transmission
- Application tasks: CoAP server, periodic beacons

Methods:
- async start() -> None
- async stop() -> None
- async send_message(dst, payload) -> bool
- get_status() -> NodeStatus

This is the main entry point for running a LICHEN node.

title: Multi-node simulation integration test
description:
Integration test: run multiple LICHEN nodes in simulation.

Test scenario:
1. Start simulator server
2. Create 5 nodes in a line topology (A-B-C-D-E)
3. Configure node A as DODAG root
4. Wait for DODAG formation (DIOs propagate, DAOs return)
5. Verify: all nodes have routes, ranks increase away from root
6. Send CoAP request from E to A
7. Verify: request arrives, response returns

Validation:
- All nodes joined DODAG
- Routing table at A has entries for B, C, D, E
- Packet from E traverses B, C, D to reach A
- Response follows source route back

Use pytest-asyncio, simulator fixtures.

This is the key validation that the stack works end-to-end.

title: Basic CoAP resources (/.well-known/core, /status)
description:
Implement basic CoAP resources for LICHEN node.

Resources:

/.well-known/core (RFC 6690):
- GET returns link-format list of resources
- Required for CoAP discovery
- Format: </resource>;rt="type";ct=0

/status:
- GET returns node status (JSON or CBOR)
- Includes: uptime, neighbors, rank, parent, battery

/config:
- GET/PUT node configuration
- Radio params, identity, network settings

/neighbors:
- GET returns neighbor table
- Address, rank, ETX, last seen

Implementation:
- Use aiocoap.resource.Resource base class
- Register with aiocoap.resource.Site
- Return appropriate content-format

CBOR encoding for efficiency (ct=60).

title: aiocoap transport binding for LICHEN
description:
Create custom aiocoap transport that uses LICHEN stack.

aiocoap supports custom transports via MessageInterface.

LichenTransport class:
- Implements aiocoap.interfaces.MessageInterface
- Sends CoAP messages through LICHEN stack (SCHC compression, link layer)
- Receives from LICHEN stack, passes to aiocoap

Integration points:
- UDP socket replacement: instead of real UDP, use our IPv6/SCHC layer
- Address mapping: CoAP addresses are IPv6 addresses
- Message serialization: aiocoap handles, we just transport

Class LichenTransport(MessageInterface):
- async send(message) -> None
- Receive callback registration
- Shutdown handling

Reference: aiocoap generic transport documentation.

title: RPL DAO handling and route advertisement
description:
Implement DAO processing for non-storing mode.

DAO sending (non-root nodes):
- Triggered by: joining DODAG, parent change, new child
- Send to DODAG root (not parent in non-storing mode)
- Contains: my prefix as target, parent as transit
- DAO-ACK expected if K flag set

DAO receiving (root):
- Parse target and transit info options
- Update routing table
- Send DAO-ACK if requested

DAO triggering:
- DAO timer after joining
- Immediate on topology change
- Periodic refresh

Class DaoManager:
- schedule_dao() -> None
- send_dao() -> None
- process_dao(dao, src) -> None (root only)
- send_dao_ack(dao, status) -> None

Test: 5-node line topology, verify root has routes to all.

title: RPL routing table and forwarding
description:
Implement RPL routing table for non-storing mode.

Non-storing mode:
- Only root has full routing table
- Other nodes just know their parent
- Downward traffic uses source routing (Routing Header)

Routing table (at root):
- Target prefix -> next hop, via path
- Built from DAO messages
- DAO contains: target prefix, transit info (parent chain)

Forwarding logic:
- Upward (toward root): send to preferred parent
- Downward (from root): insert source routing header, send to first hop
- At intermediate: pop next hop from routing header, forward

Class RoutingTable:
- add_route(target, path) -> None
- remove_route(target) -> None  
- lookup(target) -> path | None
- build_source_route(target) -> list[IPv6Address]

Source Routing Header (SRH):
- Type 3 routing header
- Segments left counter
- List of addresses to visit

title: DODAG state machine and parent selection
description:
Implement RPL DODAG formation and parent selection.

DODAG state:
- RPLInstanceID, DODAGID, Version
- Rank (16-bit, lower = closer to root)
- Preferred parent
- Parent set (backup parents)
- DODAG root flag

State machine:
- UNJOINED: send DIS, listen for DIO
- JOINED: have parent, can forward
- ROOT: I am the root (configured or elected)

Parent selection (MRHOF with ETX):
1. Receive DIO from neighbor
2. Calculate path cost = neighbor.rank + link_cost
3. If path cost < current rank - threshold: switch parent
4. Maintain parent set for redundancy

Link cost estimation:
- ETX (Expected Transmissions) from packet loss
- Initially estimate from RSSI/SNR
- Refine with actual TX success rate

Class DodagState:
- join(dio) -> None
- process_dio(dio, neighbor) -> None
- select_parent() -> None
- get_rank() -> int
- is_root() -> bool

title: Trickle timer implementation
description:
Implement Trickle algorithm per RFC 6206.

Used by RPL to control DIO transmission rate.

Parameters:
- Imin: minimum interval (e.g., 1 second)
- Imax: max doublings (e.g., 16 -> max interval 65536 seconds)
- k: redundancy constant (e.g., 10)

State:
- I: current interval
- t: time within interval to transmit
- c: consistency counter

Algorithm:
1. On interval start: c = 0, pick t random in [I/2, I)
2. On consistent transmission heard: c += 1
3. At time t: if c < k, transmit
4. At interval end: I = min(2*I, Imax * Imin)
5. On inconsistency: I = Imin, reset

Class TrickleTimer:
- __init__(imin, imax, k)
- start() -> None
- heard_consistent() -> None
- reset() -> None (on inconsistency)
- async wait_to_transmit() -> bool (should transmit?)

Uses asyncio timers.

title: RPL message types (DIO, DIS, DAO, DAO-ACK)
description:
Implement RPL control messages per RFC 6550.

ICMPv6 type 155 (RPL), codes:
- 0x00: DIS (DODAG Information Solicitation)
- 0x01: DIO (DODAG Information Object)
- 0x02: DAO (Destination Advertisement Object)
- 0x03: DAO-ACK

DIO fields:
- RPLInstanceID, Version, Rank, Flags (G, MOP, Prf)
- DTSN, DODAGID
- Options: DODAG Configuration, Prefix Information, Route Information

DAO fields:
- RPLInstanceID, Flags (K, D), DAOSequence
- DODAGID (if D flag)
- Options: RPL Target, Transit Information

DIS fields:
- Flags, Reserved
- Options: Solicited Information

Dataclasses for each message type with to_bytes/from_bytes.

title: ICMPv6 implementation (echo, errors)
description:
Implement ICMPv6 messages for basic diagnostics.

Message types:
- Echo Request (type 128)
- Echo Reply (type 129)
- Destination Unreachable (type 1)
- Packet Too Big (type 2)
- Time Exceeded (type 3)

ICMPv6 header:
- type (1 byte)
- code (1 byte)
- checksum (2 bytes)
- message body

Checksum calculation:
- Pseudo-header (src, dst, length, next_header=58)
- ICMPv6 header + body

Classes:
- ICMPv6Message base class
- EchoRequest, EchoReply
- DestinationUnreachable, etc.

Handler:
- async handle_icmpv6(packet) -> response | None
- Respond to echo requests
- Generate errors when needed

title: IPv6 packet parsing and building
description:
Implement IPv6 packet construction and parsing.

IPv6Header dataclass:
- version (always 6)
- traffic_class
- flow_label
- payload_length
- next_header
- hop_limit
- src_addr (16 bytes)
- dst_addr (16 bytes)

Methods:
- IPv6Header.to_bytes() -> bytes (40)
- IPv6Header.from_bytes(data) -> IPv6Header
- IPv6Packet (header + extension headers + payload)

Extension header support:
- Hop-by-Hop Options (RPL uses this)
- Routing Header (source routing)
- Fragment Header (if needed, probably not with SCHC)

Use ipaddress module for address handling.

title: IPv6 address handling and IID derivation
description:
Implement IPv6 address handling per spec section 7.

Address types:
- Link-local: fe80::/10 + IID
- ULA: fd00::/8 + network prefix + IID (when DODAG root present)
- GUA: 2000::/3 + prefix + IID (when border router present)

IID derivation from EUI-64:
- Insert ff:fe in middle
- Flip universal/local bit

Classes:
- Identity: holds EUI-64, derives IID, generates addresses
- AddressManager: tracks current addresses by scope

Functions:
- eui64_to_iid(eui64) -> bytes (8)
- make_link_local(iid) -> IPv6Address
- make_ula(prefix, iid) -> IPv6Address

Integration with SCHC address compression.

title: SCHC reassembly and state machine
description:
Implement SCHC reassembly state machine.

Reassembly state per (src, dst, dtag):
- fragments: dict[window][fcn] -> bytes
- windows_complete: set of complete windows
- all1_received: bool
- timer: reassembly timeout

State transitions:
- IDLE -> RECEIVING on first fragment
- RECEIVING -> COMPLETE when all fragments received
- RECEIVING -> TIMEOUT if timer expires
- COMPLETE -> IDLE after yielding packet

Memory management:
- Limit concurrent reassemblies (max 4?)
- Evict oldest on overflow
- Clear on completion or timeout

Integration with FragmentReceiver class.

Test: fragment large packet, drop some fragments, verify retransmit and reassembly.

title: SCHC fragmentation (ACK-on-Error)
description:
Implement SCHC ACK-on-Error fragmentation per RFC 8724.

Used when compressed packet > link MTU.

Fragment format:
- Rule ID (same as compression rule)
- DTAG (datagram tag, identifies fragment set)
- W (window number)
- FCN (fragment counter within window)
- Payload

Sender:
- Split packet into fragments
- Send fragments in windows
- Wait for ACK after each window
- Retransmit missing fragments from ACK bitmap
- Send All-1 fragment to indicate end

Receiver:
- Collect fragments by DTAG
- Track received FCNs per window
- Send ACK with bitmap of missing fragments
- Reassemble when All-1 received and complete

Classes:
- FragmentSender(packet, mtu) -> async iterator of fragments
- FragmentReceiver() -> async collect fragments, yield reassembled

Timeout handling for lost ACKs.

title: SCHC decompression implementation
description:
Implement SCHC decompression per RFC 8724.

decompress(context, rule_id, residue) -> packet

Algorithm:
1. Look up rule by rule_id
2. For each field descriptor:
   a. Apply decompression action
   b. Consume bits from residue as needed
   c. Reconstruct field value
3. Assemble full packet

Decompression actions:
- not-sent: use target value from rule
- value-sent: read full field from residue
- mapping-sent: read index, lookup in mapping
- LSB(n): combine with MSB from target value
- compute-checksum: calculate from reconstructed packet

BitReader class for consuming bits from residue.

Verify round-trip: decompress(compress(packet)) == packet

title: SCHC compression implementation
description:
Implement SCHC compression per RFC 8724.

compress(context, packet) -> (rule_id, residue)

Algorithm:
1. Match packet against rules in priority order
2. For matching rule:
   a. For each field descriptor:
      - Apply matching operator
      - Apply compression action
      - Accumulate sent bits into residue
3. Return rule_id + residue bitstring

Compression actions implementation:
- not-sent: emit nothing
- value-sent: emit full field
- mapping-sent: emit mapping index
- LSB(n): emit n least significant bits
- compute-checksum: compute at decompression

Bit-level operations:
- BitWriter class for accumulating bits
- Handle non-byte-aligned fields

Test with all rules from spec Appendix A.

title: SCHC rule definition and context
description:
Define SCHC rules per RFC 8724 and spec Appendix A.

Rule structure:
- rule_id: int (variable length based on rule count)
- field_descriptors: list of (field, target_value, matching_op, comp_decom_action, sent_bits)

Matching operators:
- equal, ignore, MSB(x), match-mapping

Compression/Decompression actions:
- not-sent, value-sent, mapping-sent, LSB, compute-*

Rules for LICHEN (from spec):
- Rule 0: Full IPv6 (fallback)
- Rule 1: Link-local ICMPv6
- Rule 2: ULA CoAP to root
- Rule 3: ULA CoAP from root  
- Rule 4: Multicast

Classes:
- FieldDescriptor
- Rule
- SchcContext (holds all rules, lookup by packet pattern)

Parse rules from YAML/JSON config for flexibility.

title: Link layer transmit/receive logic
description:
Implement link layer TX/RX integration.

LinkLayer class:
- __init__(radio: Radio, identity: Identity)
- async send(dst_addr, payload, security_level) -> bool
- async receive(timeout_ms) -> Frame | None
- Uses Schnorr signer for outgoing frames
- Uses ReplayProtector for incoming frames
- Tracks own sequence numbers

TX flow:
1. Build frame header
2. Sign frame (header + payload)
3. Call radio.transmit()
4. Increment seqnum

RX flow:
1. Call radio.receive()
2. Parse frame
3. Verify signature
4. Check replay protection
5. Return payload

Integration with SimRadio for testing.

title: Replay protection with sliding window
description:
Implement replay protection per spec section 4.4.

State per sender (IID):
- last_epoch: u8
- last_seqnum: u16
- window: 32-bit bitmap for recent seqnums

Algorithm:
1. Extract epoch and seqnum from frame
2. If epoch < last_epoch: reject (old epoch)
3. If epoch > last_epoch: accept, reset window, update state
4. If epoch == last_epoch:
   a. If seqnum <= last_seqnum - 32: reject (too old)
   b. If seqnum in window: reject (replay)
   c. If seqnum > last_seqnum: accept, slide window
   d. Else: accept, mark in window

Class ReplayProtector:
- check(sender_iid, epoch, seqnum) -> bool
- update(sender_iid, epoch, seqnum) -> None
- get_next_seqnum(my_iid) -> (epoch, seqnum)
- persist() / load() for epoch persistence

Unit tests for all edge cases: wrap, old epoch, replay, window edges.

title: Schnorr signature implementation
description:
Implement truncated Schnorr signatures per spec section 8.4.

Algorithm (48 bytes total):
1. Generate nonce: r = H(priv || msg) mod L
2. Compute R = r * G
3. Compute e = H(R || pub || msg), truncate to 16 bytes (e₁₂₈)
4. Compute s = r + e * priv mod L (32 bytes)
5. Signature = e₁₂₈ || s (48 bytes)

Verification:
1. Compute R' = s * G - e * pub
2. Compute e' = H(R' || pub || msg), truncate to 16 bytes
3. Verify e' == e₁₂₈

Implementation:
- Use PyNaCl for Ed25519 primitives
- Derive X25519 from Ed25519 for ECDH (EDHOC later)
- Constant-time comparison for verification

Functions:
- sign(private_key, message) -> bytes (48)
- verify(public_key, message, signature) -> bool
- keypair_generate() -> (private, public)

Test vectors from spec draft.

title: Link layer frame format dataclasses
description:
Define Python dataclasses for link layer frames per spec section 4.

Classes:
- LLSecByte: flags for addressing mode, security level
- FrameHeader: length, llsec, seqnum, dst_addr
- Frame: header + payload + signature

Address modes:
- 0: implicit (broadcast)
- 1: short (2 bytes)
- 2: extended (8 bytes)

Methods:
- Frame.to_bytes() -> bytes
- Frame.from_bytes(data) -> Frame
- Frame.max_payload_size(addr_mode) -> int

Use @dataclass(frozen=True) for immutability where appropriate.

title: Implement Zephyr native_sim radio driver for simulator
description:
Create a Zephyr radio driver for native_sim target that connects to our LoRa simulator over TCP.

This allows running the full Zephyr LICHEN stack (SCHC, RPL, CoAP) as a Linux process, with radio operations handled by our simulator.

Implementation:
1. Create drivers/lora/lora_sim.c - Zephyr LoRa driver
2. Implement Zephyr LoRa API (lora_config, lora_send, lora_recv)
3. TCP client connecting to simulator port 4444
4. Wire protocol: same binary protocol as Python SimRadio client
5. Kconfig options: CONFIG_LORA_SIM, CONFIG_LORA_SIM_HOST, CONFIG_LORA_SIM_PORT

Driver behavior:
- On init: TCP connect, send REGISTER with node_id from devicetree/Kconfig
- lora_send(): encode TX message, block until TX_DONE/TX_FAIL
- lora_recv(): encode RX message with timeout, block until RX_OK/RX_TIMEOUT
- On deinit: close TCP connection

Benefits:
- Native speed (no QEMU)
- Full debugging (gdb, valgrind, asan, coverage)
- CI-friendly (Linux binary)
- Same simulator as Python/Rust implementations

Depends on simulator being operational and wire protocol finalized.

Reference: Zephyr LoRa API - https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/hardware/peripherals/lora.html

title: Evaluate libschc for SCHC implementation
description:
Evaluate libschc (imec-idlab/libschc) for use in LICHEN.

Tasks:
1. Clone and review libschc code structure
2. Check RFC 8724 coverage - what's implemented vs missing
3. Review fragmentation support (ACK-on-Error mode we need)
4. Assess memory footprint and malloc-free operation
5. Test with our SCHC rules (Appendix A of spec)
6. Compare with CSCHC (Orange, MIT license) as alternative

Integration paths to evaluate:
- Python: Can we use libschc via cffi/ctypes, or reimplement in pure Python?
- Zephyr: Port as module (RIOT OS has done this already)
- Rust: FFI bindings or reimplement in Rust

Deliverables:
- Summary of libschc capabilities and gaps
- Recommendation: use libschc, use CSCHC, or write our own
- If using libschc: integration plan for Python simulator and Zephyr

References:
- https://github.com/imec-idlab/libschc
- https://github.com/quentinlampin/cschc
- https://doc.riot-os.org/group__pkg__libschc.html (RIOT integration)

title: Integration test: node mobility
description: Test: two nodes A and B, initially 100m apart (good link). A transmits, B receives. Move B to 5000m via REST API. A transmits, B cannot receive (out of range or very weak). Move B back to 200m. A transmits, B receives again. Verify RSSI changes match distance.

title: Integration test: chaos monkey operations
description: Test: set up 3-node network, verify baseline connectivity. Apply DropRule: verify packets dropped. Apply PartitionRule: verify cross-partition packets blocked. Apply DegradeRule: verify RSSI reduced. Apply JammerRule: verify nodes in radius can't communicate. Clear chaos, verify connectivity restored. Use REST API to apply/clear rules.

title: Integration test: collision and capture effect
description: Test: three nodes A, B, C. A and B transmit simultaneously to C. Case 1: equal power, both lost. Case 2: A is 10dB stronger, A wins (capture effect). Case 3: A is 4dB stronger, both lost (below 6dB threshold). Verify packet delivery matches expected behavior.

title: Integration test: two-node TX/RX
description: Create tests/sim/test_integration.py. Test: start simulator server, connect two SimRadio clients (nodes A and B at 100m apart), A transmits, B receives with correct RSSI/SNR. Verify: packet delivered, RSSI matches propagation model, airtime correct. Use pytest-asyncio. Fixture to start/stop simulator server.

title: Implement DutyCycleTracker
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/duty_cycle.py. DutyCycleTracker class: __init__(limit_percent=1.0, window_seconds=3600). Methods: record_tx(airtime_us, time_us), can_transmit(airtime_us, time_us) -> bool, get_usage(time_us) -> float (0.0-1.0). Sliding window: track TX times, drop entries older than window. EU 868MHz limit: 1% duty cycle. Integration: TCP server checks before allowing TX, returns TX_FAIL if exceeded. Unit tests for boundary conditions.

title: Implement simulator main entry point
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/server.py (main module). SimulatorServer class: __init__(node_port=4444, api_port=4445), async start(), async stop(). Starts both TCP node server and HTTP REST API. Manages SimulationRegistry (dict of active simulations). CLI entry point: python -m lichen.sim --node-port 4444 --api-port 4445 --log-level INFO. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Structlog for logging.

title: Implement SimRadio client
description: Create python/src/lichen/radio/sim_client.py. SimRadio class implementing Radio protocol. __init__(host, port, sim_id, node_id, position). async connect(): open TCP, send REGISTER, await OK. async transmit(payload) -> bool: send TX, await TX_DONE/TX_FAIL. async receive(timeout_ms) -> tuple | None: send RX, await RX_OK/RX_TIMEOUT. async get_time() -> int: send TIME, await TIME_OK. async close(): close TCP connection. Context manager support (__aenter__, __aexit__). Reconnection logic on disconnect. Unit tests using mock TCP server.

title: Implement pcapng packet capture
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/pcap.py. PcapngWriter class: __init__(path), write_shb() (Section Header Block), write_idb() (Interface Description Block with DLT_USER0, name='lichen-lora', custom options for SF/BW/CR), write_epb(timestamp_us, data, rssi, snr, src_node, dst_node) (Enhanced Packet Block with custom options). Use struct for binary packing. Follow pcapng spec (IETF draft-tuexen-opsawg-pcapng). Integration with Medium to capture all TX/RX events. Unit test: write file, verify with tshark -r.

title: Implement REST control API
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/api.py using Starlette. Endpoints: POST /sim (create simulation), DELETE /sim/{id}, GET /sim/{id} (status), POST /sim/{id}/tick (advance time). Node management: POST /sim/{id}/node, DELETE /sim/{id}/node/{nid}, PATCH /sim/{id}/node/{nid} (move). Chaos: POST /sim/{id}/chaos/drop, /partition, /degrade, /jam, DELETE /sim/{id}/chaos. Observe: GET /sim/{id}/packets, GET /sim/{id}/topology, WebSocket /sim/{id}/stream for live events. JSON request/response. Return appropriate HTTP status codes.

title: Implement TCP node server
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/node_server.py. asyncio TCP server on port 4444. On connect: read REGISTER message, create/lookup Simulation by sim_id, add node, send OK/ERR. Handle TX: validate, create Transmission in medium, schedule TxEndEvent, send TX_DONE with airtime. Handle RX: set node state to RX_WAIT, create RxTimeoutEvent, await packet delivery or timeout, send RX_OK or RX_TIMEOUT. Handle TIME: return current_time_us. Track connection -> node_id mapping. Clean up on disconnect.

title: Implement chaos rules framework
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/chaos.py. ChaosRule ABC with matches(tx, rx_node) -> bool and apply(tx) -> Transmission | None. Concrete rules: DropRule(node_id, direction='tx|rx|both', count), PartitionRule(groups: list[set[str]]), DegradeRule(node_id, rssi_penalty_db), JammerRule(x, y, z, radius_m, power_dbm), LatencyRule(node_id, added_us). ChaosEngine class: add_rule(rule), remove_rule(rule_id), clear(), apply_all(tx, rx_node) -> Transmission | None. Unit tests for each rule type.

title: Define wire protocol encoding/decoding
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/protocol.py. Binary protocol, little-endian. Message types: REGISTER(0x01), OK(0x00), ERR(0xFF), TX(0x10), TX_DONE(0x11), TX_FAIL(0x12), RX(0x20), RX_OK(0x21), RX_TIMEOUT(0x22), TIME(0x30), TIME_OK(0x31). Functions: encode_register(sim_id, node_id, x, y, z) -> bytes, decode_register(data) -> tuple, encode_tx(payload) -> bytes, etc. Use struct module. Comprehensive round-trip unit tests for each message type.

title: Implement Simulation class
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/simulation.py. Simulation class: id (str), nodes (dict[str, SimNode]), medium (Medium), event_queue (EventQueue), current_time_us (int), time_mode enum (BARRIER_SYNC, REALTIME). Methods: add_node(id, x, y, z) -> SimNode, remove_node(id), get_node(id), advance_to(time_us), process_next_event(), maybe_advance_time() for barrier sync (advances when all nodes in RX_WAIT). Barrier sync: deterministic, waits for all nodes to block before advancing. Realtime: advances with wall clock.

title: Implement SimNode state tracking
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/node.py. SimNode dataclass: id (str), position (tuple[float,float,float]), tx_power_dbm (default 22), state enum (IDLE, TX, RX_WAIT), pending_rx_future (for async wakeup), connected (bool), last_seen_time_us. Methods: set_position(x,y,z), disconnect(), is_online(). Position is mutable for mobility support.

title: Implement Medium class
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/medium.py. Tracks active transmissions. Methods: start_tx(node_id, payload, tx_power) -> Transmission, get_rx_candidates(node_id, position, time) -> list of (Transmission, rssi, snr). Uses PropagationModel for path loss. Collision detection: find overlapping TXs. Capture effect: if strongest signal is 6+ dB above second strongest, it wins; otherwise all colliding packets lost. Configurable noise_floor_dbm (default -120). Unit tests for: single TX delivery, collision with both lost, capture effect winner.

title: Implement Transmission and airtime calculation
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/transmission.py. Transmission dataclass: id (uuid), source_node_id, payload (bytes), tx_power_dbm, start_time_us, end_time_us, frequency_hz. Function airtime_us(payload_len: int) -> int using fixed SF10/125kHz/CR4-5 parameters. Formula: T = (2^SF / BW) * (preamble + 4.25 + 8 + max(ceil((8*PL - 4*SF + 28 + 16) / (4*SF)) * CR, 0)). Preamble=8 symbols. Unit tests with known payload sizes.

title: Implement event queue system
description: Create python/src/lichen/sim/events.py. Priority queue sorted by time. Event base class with time field. Subclasses: TxStartEvent, TxEndEvent, RxTimeoutEvent. EventQueue class: push(event), pop() -> Event, peek() -> Event | None, is_empty() -> bool. All times in microseconds (int). Unit tests for ordering, ties broken by insertion order.

title: Implement propagation model
description:
Create python/src/lichen/sim/propagation.py. Log-distance path loss: PL = PL₀ + 10·n·log₁₀(d/d₀). Default PL₀=-127.41dBm at d₀=1m for 915MHz. Configurable exponent n (2.0=free space, 2.7=urban, 3.5=indoor).

SF sensitivity thresholds from LoRaSim (Bor et al., 2016, 'Do LoRa Low-Power Wide-Area Networks Scale?'):
  SF7: -123 dBm, SF8: -126 dBm, SF9: -129 dBm, SF10: -132 dBm, SF11: -134.5 dBm, SF12: -137 dBm
  (at 125kHz BW)

Capture effect threshold: 6 dB (stronger signal wins if delta >= 6dB, per LoRaSim).

We use SF10 fixed, so threshold is -132 dBm.

Class PropagationModel with:
- path_loss(distance_m) -> float (dB)
- snr(tx_power_dbm, distance_m, noise_floor_dbm) -> float
- can_decode(snr) -> bool (snr > sensitivity - noise_floor)

Unit tests with known distances. Cite LoRaSim paper in docstring.

title: Define Radio ABC/Protocol
description: Create python/src/lichen/radio/base.py with Radio Protocol (typing.Protocol). Methods: async transmit(payload: bytes) -> bool, async receive(timeout_ms: int) -> tuple[bytes, int, int] | None (payload, rssi, snr), configure(freq_hz: int, tx_power_dbm: int) -> None. This is the interface all radio implementations must satisfy.

title: Create simulator Python project structure
description: Set up python/src/lichen/sim/ directory structure with pyproject.toml. Dependencies: anyio, starlette, uvicorn, pynacl, structlog. Dev deps: pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx. Create empty __init__.py files for: sim/, radio/, link/. This is the foundation for all simulator work.

title: Create Python project structure
description: Set up python/ directory with: pyproject.toml, src/lichen/, tests/, uv or poetry for deps. Dependencies: pynacl, aiocoap, asyncio. Type hints throughout. pytest for testing.

title: Build simulation harness
description: Python simulation runner. Features: (1) Topology definition (node positions, links), (2) Event-driven simulation loop, (3) Packet capture/logging, (4) Metrics collection (latency, delivery rate, collisions), (5) Visualization of DODAG formation. Support for reproducible runs (seeded RNG).

title: Integrate aiocoap for CoAP
description: Use aiocoap library for CoAP client/server. Integrate with LICHEN stack (SCHC compression, link layer). Implement basic resources: /.well-known/core, /status. Test request/response over simulated multi-hop mesh.

title: Implement RPL routing in Python
description: Python implementation of RFC 6550 RPL. DIO/DIS/DAO/DAO-ACK messages. Trickle timer. MRHOF objective function with ETX. DODAG formation. Non-storing mode with source routing. Test with 5+ node topology.

title: Implement minimal IPv6/6LoWPAN in Python
description: Python IPv6 packet construction/parsing. 6LoWPAN IPHC compression. Address handling: link-local, ULA, IID derivation from EUI-64. ICMPv6 echo. Integrate with SCHC layer.

title: Implement SCHC compression in Python
description: Python implementation of RFC 8724 SCHC. Implement rules 0-4 from spec Appendix A. Compression and decompression. ACK-on-Error fragmentation. Generate test vectors for each rule.

title: Implement link layer in Python
description: Python implementation of spec section 4: frame format (Length+LLSec+SeqNum+DstAddr+Payload+Sig), Ed25519 signatures (use PyNaCl), replay protection with sliding window. Generate test vectors.
close_reason: Superseded by detailed breakdown: n80, 9a9, eno, muq

title: Implement simulated radio interface
description: Python Radio class matching the trait from spec. Methods: configure(), transmit(), receive(), cad(), rssi(), snr(). Backed by wireless channel simulator. Async/event-driven design.
close_reason: Superseded by lora-ipv6-mesh-s4n (SimRadio client) in the detailed simulator plan

title: Python prototype validates protocol design
description: Gate for moving to embedded implementation. Python prototype must demonstrate: SCHC compression works, RPL DODAG forms in simulation, CoAP messaging works end-to-end, test vectors generated for Rust/C.

title: Implement replay protection
description: 16-bit sequence number with 32-entry sliding window per sender. Track per-sender SeqNum state. Reject if SeqNum <= last_seen (accounting for window).

title: Implement link layer frame format
description: Per spec section 4: Length(1B) + LLSec(1B) + SeqNum(2B) + DstAddr(2-8B) + Payload + MIC(4-8B). Implement encoding/decoding in both Rust and C. Create test vectors.

title: Implement Radio abstraction trait
description: Create Radio trait with: configure(), transmit(), receive(), cad(), rssi(), snr(). Implement for sx126x, sx127x, and simulated backends. Per spec section 3.

title: Set up Zephyr west workspace
description: Create zephyr/ directory with west.yml manifest. Set up CMakeLists.txt, Kconfig. Create board overlays for heltec_lora32_v3, rak4631, tbeam_supreme, nucleo_wl55jc. Verify west build succeeds for each board.

title: Create Rust workspace with no_std crates
description: Set up rust/ directory with Cargo.toml workspace. Create stub crates: lichen-core, lichen-link, lichen-schc, lichen-coap, lichen-senml, lichen-apps, lichen-node, lichen-gateway, lichen-sim. All core crates should be no_std by default.

title: Phase 4: RPL Routing
description: Mesh formation with MRHOF objective function. Exit criteria: 5-node mesh forms DODAG, upward and downward routing work, parent switching on link failure.

title: Phase 3: IPv6 and 6LoWPAN
description: Full IPv6 with layered addressing. Exit criteria: nodes can ping (link-local), ULA addresses work, isolated mesh self-organizes.

title: Phase 2: SCHC Compression
description: RFC 8724 SCHC for IPv6/UDP/CoAP compression. Exit criteria: all rules implemented and tested, fragmentation working, Rust and C produce identical output.

title: Phase 1: Physical & Link Layer
description: Transmit and receive authenticated frames over LoRa. Exit criteria: two nodes exchange authenticated frames, replay attack blocked, test vectors for link layer.

title: Phase 0: Python Prototype + Simulation
description: Build Python prototype with simulated wireless to validate protocol design before embedded implementation. Exit criteria: multi-node simulation runs, SCHC compression validated, RPL DODAG forms, CoAP messaging works end-to-end over simulated LoRa links.

title: Spec: Address 16-bit sequence number wrapping
description: 16-bit SeqNum with 32-entry window is weak. High-traffic nodes wrap in ~65K packets. After wrap, delayed replays become possible. Need: (1) specify behavior on wrap, (2) add epoch counter or timestamp component, (3) define state recovery after reboot. Consider 32-bit SeqNum or hybrid approach.

title: Spec: Add SOS authentication and rate limiting
description: Emergency/SOS section specifies 're-broadcast once (TTL-limited)' but no authentication. Attack vector: flood network with fake SOS messages causing DoS. Need: (1) require Ed25519 signature on SOS, (2) specify rate limiting (e.g., 1 SOS per node per hour), (3) define reputation/blacklist for SOS abusers.

title: Spec: Define OSCORE key derivation from Ed25519
description: TOFU trust model binds Ed25519 pubkeys to nodes, but OSCORE requires symmetric master secrets. How are OSCORE contexts derived from Ed25519 keypairs? Options: ECDH-style key agreement, HKDF from shared secret, pre-shared context. Must specify concrete algorithm for trust model to work end-to-end.
close_reason: Added EDHOC (RFC 9528) for OSCORE key derivation in spec/06-security.md section 8.8

title: SLIP interface for Zephyr puck
description:
Zephyr network interface driver for SLIP over USB serial.

Implements Zephyr net_if API backed by SLIP framing to USB CDC ACM.

Features:
- Standard SLIP framing (RFC 1055)
- USB CDC ACM serial
- MTU configuration
- Flow control

This allows the puck to appear as a network interface to the border router PC.

Reference: Zephyr has existing SLIP support for native_sim, adapt for USB.

title: Border router daemon (lichend)
description:
Linux daemon that implements border router functions.

Responsibilities:
- RPL DODAG root (send DIOs, process DAOs)
- IPv6 ND proxy (advertise prefixes to mesh)
- Route management (add/remove routes for mesh nodes)
- Interface with kernel routing table
- SLIP interface to LoRa puck

Implementation:
- Rust (tokio async runtime)
- Netlink for kernel route manipulation
- SLIP framing over serial
- Config file (TOML) for prefixes, interfaces, upstream
- Systemd socket activation

Config example:
```toml
[mesh]
interface = "/dev/ttyUSB0"
baud = 115200

[ipv6]
prefix = "fd00:lichen:1::/48"
upstream = "eth0"

[rpl]
instance_id = 1
mode = "non-storing"
```

Logging via tracing, journal integration.

title: [epic] Border Router Implementation
description:
6LBR (6LoWPAN Border Router) connecting LICHEN mesh to IPv6 internet.

Architecture options:

**Option A: Puck + PC (primary, start here)**
- LoRa puck (T-Beam, RAK4631) runs link layer + SCHC
- Linux PC (Pi, NUC, server) runs IPv6 routing + RPL root
- SLIP over USB serial between them
- PC has internet uplink (Ethernet, WiFi, LTE)

**Option B: Standalone (future)**
- Single device with LoRa + Ethernet/WiFi
- RAK WisGate, custom hardware, or ESP32+W5500
- Runs full stack on embedded Linux (OpenWRT/Buildroot)
- PoE for remote deployment

Core functions:
- RPL DODAG root (advertises routes into mesh)
- IPv6 ND proxy (mesh nodes get addresses)
- Prefix delegation from upstream
- NAT64/DNS64 if upstream is IPv4-only (optional)
- Resource Directory (RFC 9176) for CoAP discovery
- NTP/NTS time source for mesh

Components to build:
1. SLIP interface driver (Zephyr side)
2. SLIP interface driver (Linux side, use existing)
3. Border router daemon (RPL root, ND proxy)
4. Integration with Linux networking (ip6tables, routing)
5. systemd service files
6. Configuration management

Dependencies:
- Full LICHEN stack working on puck
- RPL implementation complete
- Local client interface defined (for SLIP framing)

Testing:
- Internet host pings mesh node
- Mesh node pings internet host
- CoAP request from internet to mesh resource
- Mesh node registers with Resource Directory

title: lichen-mobile: Mobile application (Flutter)
description:
Cross-platform mobile app for LICHEN using Flutter.

Features:
- Contact list with presence
- Messaging (1:1, group, broadcast)
- Map view with peer positions (native maps SDK)
- Waypoint creation from current location
- SOS button with confirmation
- Background operation for position beaconing
- BLE connection to LICHEN node
- QR code for contact/key exchange

Implementation:
- Flutter + Dart
- CoAP client (coap package or custom)
- BLE UART connection to node (flutter_blue_plus)
- Local notifications
- Background execution for beacons

Platform targets:
- Android (primary)
- iOS (secondary, needs BLE background mode)

Note: Phone doesn't run LICHEN stack - it's a client to a nearby LICHEN node via BLE.

title: lichen-desktop: Desktop GUI application
description:
Cross-platform desktop application for LICHEN.

Framework options:
- Tauri (Rust backend, web frontend) - smaller, faster, preferred
- Electron (if web tech sharing with mobile is priority)

Features:
- Contact list with presence indicators
- Message threads (1:1 and group)
- Map view with peer positions (OpenStreetMap/Leaflet)
- Waypoint management with map interaction
- Node configuration panel
- Key/trust management with fingerprint verification
- System tray with notifications

Implementation:
- Tauri v2 + SvelteKit or React
- CoAP over IPC to local node
- SQLite for local message cache
- Platform-native notifications

Targets: Windows, macOS, Linux.

title: lichen-tui: Terminal user interface
description:
Rust TUI for LICHEN using ratatui.

Features:
- Split pane: contacts/channels left, messages right
- Real-time message updates
- Position map (ASCII art or sixel if supported)
- Node status bar
- Keyboard-driven navigation

Implementation:
- Rust with ratatui + crossterm
- Async (tokio) for CoAP and UI
- Same CoAP client as CLI
- Config file for keybindings, colors

Target: terminal users, SSH access, low-bandwidth remote.

title: lichen-cli: Command-line interface
description:
Rust CLI for LICHEN node interaction.

Features:
- Node status and info
- Send/receive messages
- Contact management (list, add, remove, trust)
- Position sharing (send location, view peers)
- Waypoint management
- Group operations
- Key management (view fingerprints, pin/unpin)
- Configuration

Implementation:
- Rust with clap for argument parsing
- CoAP client (coap-lite or custom)
- Connects via SLIP serial or Unix socket
- JSON output mode for scripting
- Completions for bash/zsh/fish

Target: power users, scripting, headless operation.

title: [epic] Client Applications
description:
User-facing applications for LICHEN. All apps communicate with local node via IPv6+CoAP over SLIP/BLE/IPC.

CLI - lichen-cli (Rust)
TUI - lichen-tui (Rust, ratatui)
Desktop GUI - lichen-desktop (Electron or Tauri)
Mobile - lichen-mobile (Flutter)

All apps share:
- CoAP client library for node communication
- Common data models (contacts, messages, waypoints)
- Key management (TOFU pinning, contact keys)

Depends on Local Client Interface (Phase 6) being defined.

title: Write draft-lichen-link-01.md
description: IETF-style Internet-Draft for link layer. Cover: frame format, LLSec byte, addressing modes, sequence numbers, Ed25519 truncated signatures. Use RFC 2119 keywords.

title: Create test vector framework
description: Set up test/vectors/ with JSON schema for test vectors. Create initial vectors for: link layer frames, SCHC compression. Both Rust and C implementations must validate against these vectors.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI
description: Create .github/workflows/ with: Rust build/test/clippy/fmt, Zephyr build for all target boards, test vector validation. CI should run on push and PR.

title: Define shared constants
description: Create shared header/module with: frequencies per region, sync word 0x34, CoAP ports (5683, 5684), MQTT-SN port (10883), SCHC rule IDs, RPL parameters. Must be usable from both Rust and C.

title: Phase 9: Border Router
description: Full 6LBR connecting mesh to internet. Exit criteria: internet host can ping mesh node, mesh node can reach internet, Resource Directory operational.

title: Phase 8: Applications
description: Tactical radio features: messaging, position sharing, waypoints, SOS, presence, check-in, range testing, groups. Exit criteria: all P0 applications working, SOS priority handling works, group messaging works.

title: Phase 7: SenML Sensors
description: RFC 8428 SenML for all sensor data. Exit criteria: all sensor profiles implemented, observable resources work, position beaconing works.

title: Phase 6: Local Client Interface
description: IPv6+CoAP interface for phone apps and local clients. Exit criteria: SLIP framing works over serial, BLE UART works, CoAP resources implemented, client can address mesh nodes through local node.

title: Phase 5: Security Layer
description: TOFU trust model, OSCORE encryption. Exit criteria: TOFU key exchange works, OSCORE-protected CoAP works, key pinning with change detection.

title: Spec: Add store-and-forward limits and back-pressure
description: Store-and-forward has 'implementation-defined' limits — no interop guarantees. Attack: exhaust node storage with messages to offline targets. Need: mandatory minimum/maximum limits, back-pressure signaling (e.g., 5.03 Service Unavailable), memory reservation policy.
close_reason: Added storage limits (8-64 msgs, 1-16KB), FIFO+fairness eviction, 5.03 back-pressure. spec/12-apps.md section 18.1.4

title: Spec: Harden isolated mesh root election
description: 'Lowest EUI-64 wins' is deterministic but exploitable. Attacker can manufacture nodes with low EUI-64. No mechanism to demote misbehaving roots. No re-election on root failure. Consider: randomized election with proof-of-work, reputation system, election timeout, demotion protocol.
close_reason: Added root failure detection (3×Imax timeout), re-election protocol, and demotion voting (>50% threshold). spec/04-network.md

title: Spec: Define short address collision handling
description: Short address assignment says 'Hash lower 16 bits, check for collision' but no collision detection mechanism for lossy mesh. DAD over LoRa is unreliable. Race conditions when multiple nodes join. Need: concrete DAD protocol with timeouts, collision resolution, coordinator-assisted fallback.
close_reason: Added section 4.5: coordinator assigns when present, hash+DAD when isolated, signature mismatch detects collisions. spec/02-physical-link.md

title: Spec: Add congestion control for CoAP
description: No congestion control specified. CoAP CON retries over duty-cycle-limited links can cause: cascading retry storms, EU 868MHz duty cycle violations, network collapse under load. Need: adaptive retry backoff, duty cycle awareness, load shedding policy.
close_reason: Added CoAP params for LoRa (15s ACK_TIMEOUT, 2 retries), duty cycle tracking with congestion levels, 5.03 load shedding, priority queue. spec/07-transport-app.md section 10.1.1-10.1.2

title: Spec: Resolve RPL secure mode contradiction
description: Design principle: 'No pre-shared network keys'. Section 8.7: 'Recommended: Preinstalled mode with network-wide key'. These contradict. Either: (1) drop RPL secure mode recommendation, (2) use Authenticated mode with per-node keys, (3) clarify that control plane PSK is acceptable while data plane is per-peer.
close_reason: Resolved: link-layer sigs are baseline, RPL PSK is optional defense-in-depth. Updated spec/06-security.md section 8.9.

title: Spec: Define group membership protocol
description: Groups extensively documented for messaging/encryption but no join/leave protocol. Missing: membership synchronization, authorization model, key distribution mechanism (currently 'out-of-band'). Need concrete protocol for group CRUD operations and member management.
close_reason: Added section 18.8.2: roles (owner/admin/member), invitations, key distribution via pairwise OSCORE, leave/remove, rekeying on removal. spec/12-apps.md

title: Spec: Define OTA firmware update mechanism
description: No firmware update mechanism specified. Critical for IoT security — devices need patches. Consider: CoAP block-wise transfer for images, SUIT manifest (RFC 9019), secure boot verification, rollback protection. This is essential for production deployment.

title: Spec: Add SCHC rule versioning
description: SCHC rules are pre-provisioned with no versioning scheme. Problems: (1) no fallback when rules mismatch, (2) firmware updates could silently break interop, (3) no error detection for rule mismatch. Need: rule version field in packets, negotiation/fallback mechanism, or minimum version advertisement in DIO.
close_reason: Added section 5.7: DIO advertises rule version, Rule 255 reserved for uncompressed fallback. spec/03-adaptation.md

title: Outreach: ARDC IPv6 allocation for amateur LICHEN meshes
description:
Explore getting IPv6 address space from ARDC (Amateur Radio Digital Communications) for licensed amateur radio operators running LICHEN meshes.

Background:
- AMPRNet (44.0.0.0/8) is the amateur radio IPv4 allocation
- ARDC manages it and is working on IPv6 allocation from ARIN
- A slice of that space for LICHEN would provide globally routable addresses

What we'd ask for:
- /48 or /44 from the amateur radio IPv6 allocation
- For border routers operated by licensed amateurs
- Enables real GUA addresses instead of just ULA

The pitch:
- LICHEN is standards-based (IPv6/RPL/CoAP/SCHC)
- Auth-only model is Part 97 compatible (no encryption hiding content)
- Working spec and implementation
- Border routers can announce prefixes, route to mesh

Benefits:
- Legitimacy in ham community
- Real globally routable addresses
- Potential ARDC grant funding for development
- Ties LICHEN to established amateur radio infrastructure

GRANT OPPORTUNITY:
ARDC funds open source amateur radio projects ($10k-$500k range). Once we have working code + small deployment, apply for grant covering:
- Hardware for test deployments
- Developer time
- Documentation
- Integration with ARDC IPv6 allocation

Prerequisites:
- Working border router implementation
- Part 97 compliance documentation
- Small real-world test deployment (5-10 nodes)
- Relationship building with ARDC

Timing: 6-12 month process, start after core implementation is stable.

References:
- https://www.ardc.net/
- https://www.ampr.org/
- https://www.ardc.net/apply/

title: Phase 10: Tooling & Polish
description: lichen-craft CLI, Wireshark dissector, network simulator, web dashboard. Exit criteria: all tools functional, documentation complete, example applications working.

title: Spec: Complete position privacy model
description: 'group' privacy mode says 'query requires auth' but no mechanism defined. Beacons still reveal presence to passive eavesdroppers. Group membership undefined. Need: auth protocol for position queries, beacon encryption option, group membership definition.
close_reason: Added query auth (OSCORE required), group beacon encryption, private mode whitelist, documented presence vs location limitation. spec/12-apps.md section 18.2.4

title: Spec: Address link-layer metadata exposure
description: Spec acknowledges 'Metadata visible: Link-layer headers unencrypted' but doesn't mitigate. Enables: traffic analysis, node tracking via IID/EUI-64, network topology discovery by passive adversaries. Consider: IID rotation, traffic padding, dummy messages for cover traffic.
close_reason: Not a concern per threat model: auth-only, not concealment. Running in the clear is intentional.

title: Spec: Define CoAP block-wise vs SCHC fragmentation interaction
description: Both CoAP block-wise (RFC 7959) and SCHC (RFC 8724) fragment large payloads. Interaction unspecified: which layer fragments first? How do reassembly failures propagate? What's the overhead when both active? Need clear layering rules and failure handling.
close_reason: CoAP block-wise NOT RECOMMENDED. SCHC handles all fragmentation. Large transfers use app-level chunking. spec/07-transport-app.md section 10.3
